style="text;html=1;whiteSpace=wrap;overflow=hidden;rounded=0;fontSize=15;" value="<h1 style="margin-top: 0px;">Function selection</h1><div><br></div><div>At the function selecting phase there is a list with all available names of functions to select. The LLM is aware of those function and will automatically try to pick a appropriate one. In that process we look at the token the LLM selects decode it and parse them character by character.&nbsp;</div><div>We will autocomplete a functions name if the given string can only match to one div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 558px; height: 1px; padding-top: 477px; margin-left: 412px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 13px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 288px; height: 1px; padding-top: 12px; margin-left: 362px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; max-height: 236px; overflow: hidden; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 13px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
div.h1: No keys are generated by the LLM
div.p: Keys are injected into the prompt and JSON output.

This ensures proper JSON structure and reduces token usage. A downside of it is that it is less flexible as it needs a JSON schema to be reading from, as this project is about a predefined JSON schema (Tool calling) it fits the requirement.

path.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 288px; height: 1px; padding-top: 7px; margin-left: 662px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; max-height: 196px; overflow: hidden; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 13px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
div.h1: Values are restricted to function definition
div.p: Values are checked by the FSM Nodes to ensure that the LLM is not using wrong data types.
If the the argument "a" in a function as the type int a int FSM-node will be created to insure correct type handling,

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 118px; height: 1px; padding-top: 332px; margin-left: 506px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: LLM can only pick a name from a ristricted function name list

path.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 358px; height: 1px; padding-top: 7px; margin-left: 2px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; max-height: 246px; overflow: hidden; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 15px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
div.div: At the function selecting phase there is a list with all available names of functions to select. The LLM is aware of those function and will automatically try to pick a appropriate one. In that process we look at the token the LLM selects decode it and parse them character by character.
We will autocomplete a functions name if the given string can only match to one function in particular.
